steps.title: Compute the volume and solve for k
steps.content: Using the volume formula $$ V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h $$ with $$ r = 14 $$ and $$ h = 6 $$, we get $$ V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (14)^2 (6) = \frac{1}{3}\pi \cdot 196 \cdot 6 = \frac{1}{3}\pi \cdot 1176 = 392\pi $$. Hence $$ k = 392 $$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
